meta.title: The Iron Emir: Timur's Legacy
meta.description: The last great nomadic conqueror stands at the crossroads of history. 🗡️🌍 Will he turn East to China, stabilize his empire, or crush Europe?
meta.historical_context: Emir Timur (Tamerlane) was the most powerful ruler of the 14th century. His empire was a military juggernaut but lacked administrative stability.

emir-timur.name: Emir Timur (Tamerlane)
emir-timur.description: The last of the great nomadic conquerors. A military genius who sought to restore the Mongol Empire. 🗡️👑

shahrukh.name: Shah Rukh
shahrukh.description: Timur's youngest son who turned the empire from a war machine into a center of art and science. 🎨📚

ulugh-beg.name: Ulugh Beg
ulugh-beg.description: A ruler who preferred stars to swords. Built one of the greatest observatories in history in Samarkand. 🔭✨

immortal-emir.title: The Immortal Emir: Conquest of China 🐉⚔️
immortal-emir.description: What if Timur survived his illness in 1405 and led his massive army into China?
immortal-emir.divergence_description: Timur recovers from his illness in Otrar and launches the invasion of Ming China.
immortal-emir.present_day_status: The Great Timurid Federation spans from Turkey to Korea. Samarkand is the world's capital of science and culture. Europe is a collection of backward feudal states. The primary global language is Chagatai Turkic. 🕌🌍

survival-1405.title: The Miracle at Otrar
survival-1405.description: Timur recovers from his winter illness. The army marches East instead of dispersing.
survival-1405.impact: The campaign against China proceeds as planned.

fall-of-beijing-1407.title: The Fall of Beijing
fall-of-beijing-1407.description: Timurid forces, utilizing superior cavalry and gunpowder, breach the Great Wall and sack Beijing.
fall-of-beijing-1407.impact: End of the Ming Dynasty. Establishment of the Timurid Dynasty in China.

silk-road-monopoly-1410.title: The Silk Road Monopoly
silk-road-monopoly-1410.description: With control from Anatolia to the Pacific, Timur establishes absolute control over the Silk Road.
silk-road-monopoly-1410.impact: Europe is starved of trade, delaying the Renaissance. Central Asia becomes the world's economic hub.

asian-century.short_term: Central Asia becomes the wealthiest region on Earth.
asian-century.long_term: Global power center remains in Asia. European colonialism never starts.
asian-century.global_impact: A completely different modern world dominated by Turko-Mongol culture.

no-ottoman-empire.trigger: Timur's continued dominance prevents Ottoman recovery
no-ottoman-empire.consequence: The Ottoman Empire remains a minor beylik or is absorbed.

unified-succession.title: The Pax Timuridea: A Stable Empire ⚖️🏛️
unified-succession.description: What if Timur established a clear succession plan that prevented civil war?
unified-succession.divergence_description: Timur designates a clear heir and administrative structure before his death.
unified-succession.present_day_status: The Timurid Commonwealth is a constitutional monarchy and the world's leading superpower. It is known for its beautiful garden-cities and advanced space program (launched from the Baikonur Cosmodrome, a Timurid royal project). 🚀🌌

council-of-samarkand-1404.title: The Council of Samarkand
council-of-samarkand-1404.description: Timur gathers his sons and generals, establishing a formal law of succession and federal administration.
council-of-samarkand-1404.impact: Prevents the internecine wars that historically destroyed the empire.

timurid-renaissance-1450.title: The Timurid Renaissance
timurid-renaissance-1450.description: Stable rule allows art, astronomy, and mathematics to flourish earlier and more intensely than in Europe.
timurid-renaissance-1450.impact: Scientific revolution begins in Central Asia in the 15th century.

scientific-islam.long_term: Islam becomes associated primarily with science and progress, not conservatism.
scientific-islam.global_impact: Modern science has its roots in Samarkand, not Italy or England.

industrial-samarkand.trigger: Early scientific method leads to early industrialization
industrial-samarkand.consequence: Industrial revolution begins in Central Asia in 1700s.

western-campaign.title: The Scourge of Europe ⚔️🏰
western-campaign.description: What if Timur turned West to finish off Europe instead of China?
western-campaign.divergence_description: Timur decides the "Franks" are a greater threat than the Ming.
western-campaign.present_day_status: Europe is a collection of poor, agrarian vassal states of the Golden Horde. The Americas are dominated by the modernized Aztec and Inca Federations. The world is multipolar and vastly different. 🌎🏛️

invasion-of-europe-1403.title: The Invasion of Europe
invasion-of-europe-1403.description: After defeating the Ottomans, Timur crosses the Bosphorus into Europe.
invasion-of-europe-1403.impact: Eastern Europe is devastated. Hungary and Austria fall.

sack-of-rome-1405.title: The Sack of Rome
sack-of-rome-1405.description: Timurid forces reach Rome. The Papacy is dismantled.
sack-of-rome-1405.impact: Collapse of Catholic authority. Europe fractures into small heresies and warlord states.

dark-ages-return.short_term: European Renaissance is strangled in the cradle.
dark-ages-return.long_term: Europe remains a technological backwater for centuries.
dark-ages-return.global_impact: No Age of Discovery, no colonization of the Americas by Europe.

aztec-survival.consequence: Aztec and Inca empires survive and modernize independently.

ankara-strategy.title: The Battle of Ankara (1402)
ankara-strategy.text: The Ottoman Sultan Bayezid I stands before you. His army is exhausted from a forced march, but his Janissaries are fresh. Your scouts report a stream that supplies their camp. What is your command?

cut-water.text: Divert the stream. Force them to fight thirsty.
cut-water.consequence: The Ottomans panic. Their Tatar allies defect. You win a crushing victory.

direct-charge.text: Charge the Janissaries directly with war elephants.
direct-charge.consequence: The elephants cause chaos, but the Janissaries hold. The battle turns into a bloodbath.

feigned-retreat.text: Feign a retreat to draw them out.
feigned-retreat.consequence: Bayezid is wary but his commanders are rash. They break formation, but your ambush is late.

pyrrhic-victory.title: A Costly Triumph (1402)
pyrrhic-victory.text: You have won, but at a terrible cost. Thousands of your best cavalry are dead. Bayezid escaped. The road to the West is open, but your army is bleeding.

consolidate.text: Halt the campaign. Return to Samarkand to rebuild.
consolidate.consequence: You secure your empire but lose momentum. Europe is spared.

raid-pillage.text: Raid Anatolia for loot to pay mercenaries.
raid-pillage.consequence: You burn cities to the ground. Your treasury fills, but your legacy is one of terror.

defense-of-samarkand.title: Retreat to the Oxus (1403)
defense-of-samarkand.text: The Ankara campaign failed. Bayezid is countering. You have retreated to your capital, Samarkand. The enemy is approaching the Oxus river.

scorched-earth.text: Burn the fields. Leave them nothing but ash.
scorched-earth.consequence: The Ottoman army starves in the desert. You survive, but your lands are ruined.

diplomatic-peace.text: Sue for peace. Offer tribute to Bayezid.
diplomatic-peace.consequence: History laughs at the "Great" Timur who paid tribute. Your empire crumbles from within.

otrar-crisis.title: The Winter at Otrar (1405)
otrar-crisis.text: It is February 1405. You lie dying in your tent. The Chinese border is weeks away. The campaign of a lifetime hangs in the balance.

elixir.text: Take the alchemist's experimental mercury elixir.
elixir.consequence: Fire courses through your veins. You awake, stronger than ever. The invasion proceeds!

write-will.text: Accept fate. Dictate a clear law of succession.
write-will.consequence: You name an heir and a council. You die peacefully, leaving a stable roadmap.

turn-west.text: Order the army to turn back West. "China is too far."
turn-west.consequence: The generals are confused but obedient. The army marches back towards Europe.

great-wall.title: Breaching the Great Wall (1406)
great-wall.text: The Ming defenses are formidable. The Great Wall stands before you. Your artillery is positioned. How do you breach?

concentrated-fire.text: Concentrate all gunpowder artillery on one gate.
concentrated-fire.consequence: The gate shatters. The Timurid heavy cavalry pours through. The road to Beijing is open.

bribe-general.text: Send gold to the Ming border commander.
bribe-general.consequence: The gates open at night. You take the fort without a fight.

bosphorus-crossing.title: Crossing the Bosphorus (1406)
bosphorus-crossing.text: You have returned to Anatolia. Europe lies across the water. The Venetians offer ships... for a price.

pay-venice.text: Pay the Venetians to ferry your army.
pay-venice.consequence: You land in Thrace. Europe trembles.

build-ships.text: Force the locals to build a bridge of boats.
build-ships.consequence: It takes months, but you cross on your own terms. The shock is absolute.

succession-crisis.title: The Council of Princes (1405)
succession-crisis.text: Timur is dead. The will is read, but ambition runs deep. Pir Muhammad is the heir, but he is far away.

enforce-will.text: Back Pir Muhammad with the elite guard.
enforce-will.consequence: The other princes back down... for now. A fragile peace holds.

seize-power.consequence: Civil war erupts immediately. The empire burns.

ulugh-beg-dilemma.title: The Scholar on the Throne (1449)
ulugh-beg-dilemma.text: Decades later. Ulugh Beg rules in Samarkand. His son is rebelling, claiming the King cares more for stars than god.

fight-hard.consequence: You win, but the treasury is empty. The Renaissance continues.

abdicate.consequence: You are executed. The observatory is destroyed.
